---
title: ""
page-layout: full
---
::: {.project-header}
::: {.container}
[← Back to Research](../research.qmd){.back-link}
# AI Regulation in the U.S. Acting Industry
::: {.project-meta}
<span><strong>Course:</strong> Government and Regulation</span>
<span><strong>Instructor:</strong> Prof. Ruixue Jia</span>
<span><strong>Term:</strong> Spring 2026</span>
<span><strong>Tools:</strong> R · BLS QCEW Data</span>
:::
:::
:::
::: {.container style="max-width: 860px; margin: 0 auto; padding-bottom: 5rem;"}

## Overview

Policy analysis using original BLS QCEW data to quantify AI's emerging impact on performing arts employment. Analyzes regulatory capture dynamics in the SAG-AFTRA context and argues that collective bargaining is structurally insufficient as the primary AI governance mechanism — a federal statutory baseline is required.

*Full write-up coming soon.*

:::
